message:
🐛 Fix deduplicatedClusters safety: guard kubectl on unreachable contexts (#11147)

Root cause: deduplicateClustersByServer aggregated reachability across
aliases but kept the primary context name based on friendliness/length.
If the primary was unreachable but an alias worked, kubectl operations
would silently target the unusable context.

Fix: Add reachability as the highest-priority sort criterion when
selecting the primary context. Reachable contexts now sort first,
ensuring cluster.name refers to a usable context for kubectl operations.

Also:
- ComplianceReports cluster picker now filters to reachable clusters only
- ControlPlaneHealth filters unreachable clusters from health determination

Signed-off-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
